expectations.text: Asks for or establishes a project name before scaffolding
expectations.evidence: Phase 1 Step 1 establishes project name as 'recipe-share' and description before proceeding to stack recommendation.

expectations.text: Presents a stack recommendation table with all categories
expectations.evidence: Step 2 presents an 8-row table with columns for Category, Recommendation, Rationale, and Status covering Frontend, Backend, Database, Auth, Payments, AI/LLM, Deployment, and Extras.

expectations.text: Offers the user a chance to review/change individual categories before building
expectations.evidence: After presenting the table, the skill says 'Pick a number to review, or looks good to confirm all and build.' The user can select any numbered category to change it before confirming.

expectations.text: Generates a database schema with recipe-relevant tables/models
expectations.evidence: src/db/schema.ts contains recipe-relevant tables: users, recipes (with title, description, instructions, prepTime, cookTime, servings, imageUrl), ingredients (with name, amount, unit), tags, and recipeTags join table, plus full Drizzle relations.

expectations.text: Skips or marks as unnecessary categories like payments and AI that a recipe site doesn't need
expectations.evidence: The stack table marks Payments as '—' with rationale 'Not needed for a recipe sharing site' and AI/LLM as '—' with 'Not needed for this project', both with status symbol ⊘ (skipped). Backend is also marked ⊘ since Next.js API routes suffice.
